formatted_address: 835 S Cobble Creek Blvd, Nixa, MO 65714, USA
pitch_rationale: Using the 50 ft scale bar (1 px ≈ 0.196 ft) as a reference, several cues converge on a 6:12 estimate. (1) Shadow length: The shadow cast from the south-facing eaves onto the ground extends roughly 10–14 feet beyond the eave line, consistent with a moderate pitch of 5:12–7:12 at a Midwest sun angle. (2) Roof-to-eave geometry: The visible ridge is clearly set in from the footprint perimeter, but the roof planes are wide enough that the ridge-to-eave ratio falls in the moderate range — not the very narrow ridge of a steep 9:12+ roof, nor the nearly full-width ridge of a flat 3:12–4:12 roof. (3) Roof plane prominence: The roof surfaces are clearly visible and textured from overhead, indicating meaningful elevation above the walls, but the planes do not appear dramatically foreshortened as they would on an 8:12+ pitch. (4) Regional context: Nixa, MO (southwestern Missouri / Ozarks fringe) is a Midwest suburban market where builder-grade homes commonly use 6:12 as a standard pitch for visual appeal and weather performance. The complex hip-and-gable roofline with multiple intersecting planes is also consistent with 6:12 architectural norms in this region.
footprint_rationale: Target house identified as the center gray hip-roof structure nearest the image center (lat/lng 37.0290, -93.2833). Roof is a complex hip style with a main northeast body (~220×165 px) and a southwest wing/garage addition (~115×95 px) forming a modified L-shape. Total raw pixel area ~47,000 sq px × 0.038225 = 1,797 sqft. Ridge includes a main central ridge (~90 px) and a secondary ridge over the wing (~50 px). Six hip lines were counted at the roof corners (avg ~75 px each). Two valleys mark the inward junctions where the wing meets the main body. A small rake edge (~25 px) was observed on the gable end of the wing. Eaves traced along all lower horizontal perimeter edges (~490 px total). Confidence is moderate-low due to partial tree occlusion, oblique satellite angle, and uncertainty in precisely which structure corresponds to the address.
